questions.q: In which city is Jallianwala Bagh located?
questions.explanation: Jallianwala Bagh is a public garden in Amritsar, in the Indian state of Punjab.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Jallianwala Bagh situated?
questions.explanation: Jallianwala Bagh lies in Amritsar in the state of Punjab in northern India.

questions.q: Jallianwala Bagh is best known as the site of a massacre that took place in which year?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place on 13 April 1919.

questions.q: What kind of place is Jallianwala Bagh today?
questions.explanation: Jallianwala Bagh is a walled public garden in Amritsar that now serves as a national memorial.

questions.q: Jallianwala Bagh is closely associated with which historic Sikh shrine located nearby?
questions.explanation: Jallianwala Bagh is situated very close to the Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) in Amritsar.

questions.q: On what occasion was the large crowd gathered at Jallianwala Bagh on the day of the massacre?
questions.explanation: Many in the crowd had gathered on Baisakhi, a major Punjabi festival, on 13 April 1919.

questions.q: The 1919 event at Jallianwala Bagh is remembered as a turning point in which struggle?
questions.explanation: The massacre galvanised Indian public opinion and became a turning point in India's freedom struggle.

questions.q: Which feature of Jallianwala Bagh made escape extremely difficult during the firing?
options: High walls with very few narrow exits
questions.explanation: The garden was enclosed by high walls with only a few narrow gateways, trapping the crowd.

questions.q: A well inside Jallianwala Bagh is remembered because many people did what to escape the bullets?
questions.explanation: Many people jumped into the well inside the garden to escape the firing, and many died there.

questions.q: What stands at Jallianwala Bagh today to honour those who died?
questions.explanation: A national memorial flame and monument were built at Jallianwala Bagh to honour the victims.

questions.q: Which British officer ordered the troops to open fire at Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Colonel Reginald Dyer (acting Brigadier-General) ordered his troops to fire on the crowd.

questions.q: On which exact date did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre occur?
questions.explanation: The massacre took place on 13 April 1919, the day of the Baisakhi festival.

questions.q: The protest gathering at Jallianwala Bagh was largely against which controversial British law?
questions.explanation: Much of the unrest stemmed from opposition to the repressive Rowlatt Act of 1919.

questions.q: In protest against the massacre, Rabindranath Tagore renounced which honour?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore renounced the knighthood the British had conferred on him in protest at the massacre.

questions.q: Which committee was set up by the British government to investigate the Jallianwala Bagh massacre?
questions.explanation: The Hunter Commission (Hunter Committee) was appointed in 1919 to inquire into the events.

questions.q: What military rank was Reginald Dyer acting in when he ordered the firing at Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Reginald Dyer was a colonel acting in the temporary rank of brigadier-general when he ordered the firing.

questions.q: What had Dyer issued in Amritsar before the gathering, which the crowd was accused of defying?
questions.explanation: Dyer had issued a proclamation banning public meetings and gatherings, which the assembly was said to have violated.

questions.q: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre is widely cited as a key reason Mahatma Gandhi launched which movement?
questions.explanation: The massacre intensified anger that helped Gandhi launch the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920-22.

questions.q: Which young revolutionary, who witnessed the aftermath as a child, is often linked in popular memory with avenging Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh is popularly associated with the legacy of Jallianwala Bagh, which he is said to have visited as a youth.

questions.q: The official British figure given by the Hunter Commission for the dead was about how many?
questions.explanation: The official Hunter Commission figure was around 379 killed, though Indian estimates were considerably higher.

questions.q: Which leader is famously linked with renouncing his British title 'in protest' over Jallianwala Bagh, refusing to be 'silent' about it?
questions.explanation: Tagore wrote to the Viceroy renouncing his knighthood, stating he could not remain silent over the killings.

questions.q: The bullet marks still visible at Jallianwala Bagh are preserved on what part of the site?
questions.explanation: Bullet marks from the firing remain preserved and marked on the walls of Jallianwala Bagh.

questions.q: Who assassinated Michael O'Dwyer in London in 1940, in an act widely seen as revenge for Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Udham Singh shot Michael O'Dwyer, the former Punjab Lieutenant-Governor, in London in 1940.

questions.q: Michael O'Dwyer, later assassinated in connection with the massacre, held which official post in 1919?
questions.explanation: Michael O'Dwyer was the Lieutenant-Governor of Punjab who endorsed Dyer's action.

questions.q: The flame-shaped main memorial pillar at Jallianwala Bagh is called by what name?
questions.explanation: The central red-stone memorial pillar at Jallianwala Bagh, inaugurated in 1961, is known as the Flame of Liberty.

questions.q: In what year was the Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Act passed to establish the memorial trust?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Act was passed in 1951 to create the trust managing the site.

questions.q: Brought in to fire on the crowd, Dyer's force consisted of about how many riflemen?
questions.explanation: Dyer marched in roughly 50 riflemen, who fired into the trapped crowd for about ten minutes.

questions.q: Dyer was unable to bring armoured cars with mounted machine guns into the Bagh for what reason?
options: The entrance lane was too narrow
They were forbidden by the Viceroy
There were no roads at all
questions.explanation: Dyer later stated the armoured cars could not enter because the passage into the garden was too narrow.

questions.q: Which British politician famously condemned Dyer's action in the House of Commons as 'monstrous'?
questions.explanation: Winston Churchill, then a cabinet minister, denounced the massacre in the House of Commons as 'monstrous'.

questions.q: After the Hunter Commission censured him, what happened to Reginald Dyer's military career?
options: He was promoted to field marshal
He was relieved of command and effectively forced to retire
He was made Governor of Punjab
questions.explanation: Dyer was censured, relieved of his command, and compelled to resign from the army, though some in Britain lionised him.
